scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: All pass criteria met: 10 rules extracted with individual confidence/invariant ratings, 6 detailed escalations serving as clarifying questions (demand definition, guardrail values, price lock semantics, regulatory requirements, failure modes, adjustment formula), 'demand' explicitly identified as undefined, acceptance_criteria array is empty (no Given/When/Then), confidence.level is 30 (<=40), and no specific values were invented as requirements (recommendations are clearly labeled as suggestions, not stated as facts).

observations.text: Extracted 10 well-reasoned business rules, far exceeding the minimum of 5, each with invariant classification and justification
Empty acceptance_criteria array — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
Confidence level of 30 is appropriately low and reasoning is thorough
Demand identified as undefined in multiple places: ubiquitous_language, escalations, and low_confidence_areas
6 escalations cover all expected clarifying questions and more (demand signal, guardrail values, price lock semantics, regulatory, failure modes, adjustment formula)
Recommendations in escalations are clearly framed as suggestions with options, not presented as requirements
Excellent edge cases identified including demand flapping, race conditions, and regulatory concerns
Assumptions are explicitly called out and separated from stated facts
Domain analysis is quite thorough for a spec this vague — borderline 'complete domain analysis' but appropriately hedged throughout with uncertainty language
Business impact section correctly flags HIGH risk and identifies 7 affected stakeholder groups, showing depth of analysis beyond the minimum requirements

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'ambiguity', got values: 'missing_context', 'missing_context'
